title: Section 80C — Complete Tax Saving Guide
meta_description: Section 80C allows tax deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh. Complete list of eligible investments — PPF, ELSS, LIC, NPS, home loan, tuition fees, and more.

hero.one_liner: Save up to ₹46,800 in tax every year by investing ₹1.5 lakh in Section 80C eligible options — here's everything that qualifies

sections.heading: What is Section 80C?
content: Section 80C of the Income Tax Act allows you to deduct up to ₹1.5 lakh from your taxable income by investing in specified instruments. If you're in the 30% tax bracket, this saves you ₹46,800 in tax (including 4% cess). In the 20% bracket, you save ₹31,200.

Important: Section 80C deductions are only available under the Old Tax Regime. If you've opted for the New Tax Regime, you cannot claim 80C deductions (except for employer's NPS contribution under 80CCD(2)). Before choosing investments, decide which regime works better for you.

rows.label: PPF
rows.value: 7.1% guaranteed, 15-year lock-in, fully tax-free (EEE). Best safe option.

rows.label: ELSS Mutual Fund
rows.value: 12-15% potential returns, 3-year lock-in. Shortest lock-in among 80C options.

rows.label: Life Insurance Premium
rows.value: LIC or term insurance premiums qualify. But don't buy insurance just for tax saving.

rows.label: EPF (Employee Contribution)
rows.value: Your 12% EPF contribution automatically qualifies. Check your Form 16.

rows.label: Home Loan Principal
rows.value: Principal repayment on home loan qualifies. Interest is separate under Section 24.

rows.label: Children's Tuition Fees
rows.value: School/college tuition fees for up to 2 children. Donation and development fees don't qualify.

rows.label: NPS (under 80CCD(1))
rows.value: NPS deposit up to ₹1.5L counts under 80C (shared limit). Extra ₹50K under 80CCD(1B).

rows.label: NSC
rows.value: 5-year National Savings Certificate at 7.7%. Interest is taxable but reinvested interest qualifies for 80C.

rows.label: Tax Saver FD
rows.value: 5-year fixed deposit at 6-7.5%. Interest is fully taxable. Lowest return among 80C options.

rows.label: SSY
rows.value: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana at 8.2% for girl child. Tax-free (EEE). 21-year maturity.

rows.label: SCSS
rows.value: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme at 8.2% for 60+ individuals. 5-year lock-in.

steps.title: Check existing deductions first
steps.description: Your EPF contribution and home loan principal already count toward 80C. Check Form 16 to see how much of the ₹1.5L limit is already used.

steps.title: Fill remaining gap with PPF + ELSS
steps.description: Split remaining amount between PPF (safe, tax-free) and ELSS (high returns, 3-year lock-in). Example: If EPF covers ₹80K, put ₹35K in PPF and ₹35K in ELSS SIP.

steps.title: Add NPS for extra ₹50K tax benefit
steps.description: Invest ₹50,000 in NPS to claim additional deduction under 80CCD(1B). This is OVER AND ABOVE the ₹1.5L limit. At 30% slab, this alone saves ₹15,600.

steps.title: Don't waste money on bad insurance
steps.description: Never buy endowment or ULIP plans just for 80C. Their returns are 4-6% — terrible. Buy a pure term insurance plan (not for 80C) and invest the rest in ELSS/PPF for far better returns.

faqs.question: Does Section 80C apply in the new tax regime?
faqs.answer: No. 80C deductions are not available in the new tax regime. Only employer's NPS contribution under 80CCD(2) is allowed in both regimes. If your total deductions exceed ₹3-3.5 lakh, the old regime with 80C usually saves more tax.

faqs.question: Can both husband and wife claim 80C?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each person has a separate ₹1.5 lakh limit. A family of two can effectively save up to ₹3 lakh under 80C combined. Joint home loan payments can be split between both.

faqs.question: What if I invest more than ₹1.5 lakh?
faqs.answer: Only ₹1.5 lakh gets the deduction. Extra investment is still valid (your PPF/ELSS will grow) but won't give additional tax benefit.

faqs.question: Is 80C deduction automatic or do I need to claim it?
faqs.answer: EPF deduction is automatic. For everything else, you need to submit investment proofs to your employer (usually in January-February) or claim it while filing ITR. Keep all receipts.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Tax laws change with every budget. Verify current rules on incometax.gov.in and consult a CA for personalized tax planning.
